summary.intent: Fix code review comments from PR #481 on the moltis repo (flagged by greptile bot) and resolve the review conversations.
summary.outcome: All three greptile bot comments were addressed by editing server.rs, lib.rs, and main.rs; changes were compiled, tested, committed, and pushed with conversations resolved.

repo: The TLS crate uses a custom Context trait re-exported from its own error module via impl_context!() macro, not anyhow::Context — so .with_context() works but is the crate's own implementation.
The TLS crate's Result type is its own, not anyhow::Result; AddrParseError has a From impl allowing .parse()? to work directly, but loses context on error.
Project uses worktrees under /Users/penso/.superset/worktrees/moltis/<branch-name>/ for isolated branch development.

code.finding: AddrParseError has a From impl enabling ? operator without explicit context, but .with_context() on the crate's custom Context trait also works for richer errors.
Does not import anyhow directly; uses a custom Context trait from its own error module that provides .with_context(|| format!(...)) accepting FnOnce() -> C where C: Into<String>.

workflow: greptile bot posts automated review comments on PRs; these can be fixed and resolved in a single follow-up commit.
Verifying compilation before committing is important when changing error-handling patterns across crates with different Result/Context types.

friction: The TLS crate's custom Context trait (not anyhow::Context) caused ambiguity — required reading error.rs and grepping for impl_context macro to confirm .with_context() would compile.
No anyhow import in the TLS crate made it non-obvious whether standard anyhow patterns would work there.
